accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: This Accessibility Statement describes the accessibility status of riccardobozzato.com under Italian law (D.Lgs. 104/2022) and the European Union (Directive 2019/882). This website is committed to ensuring accessibility for all users, regardless of their abilities or technologies used.

sections.title: 1. Compliance Status
sections.content: riccardobozzato.com is partially compliant with the WCAG 2.1 Level AA standard (Web Content Accessibility Guidelines). The website was designed with accessibility in mind, but some elements may not fully meet all criteria.

sections.title: 2. Applicable Legal Requirements
sections.content:
This statement is issued under:

• D.Lgs. 104/2022 (Implementation of EU Directive 2019/882) — accessibility obligations for websites and mobile applications of public and private entities.
• AgID Regulation (Agency for Digital Italy) — guidelines for websites and mobile applications of public bodies.
• Directive 2019/882 — European directive on the accessibility of digital products and services.

sections.title: 3. WCAG 2.1 Level AA Criteria
sections.content:
The four WCAG principles are:

Perceivable: all text content available as text; images with text alternatives; sufficient color contrast; non-text content with text alternatives.

Operable: functions navigable via keyboard; sufficient time to read and use content; no content that causes seizures.

Understandable: text readable and understandable; predictable appearance and behavior; assistance in form completion.

Robust: content compatible with current and future assistive technologies; names, roles, and values of interface elements correctly defined.

sections.title: 4. Accessibility Measures Implemented
sections.content:
• Semantic HTML structure with correct heading hierarchy (h1 → h2 → h3).
• Full keyboard navigation for all interactive elements.
• Visible focus indicators on all interactive elements (:focus-visible).
• Alternative text (alt text) on all informative images.
• Reduced motion support (prefers-reduced-motion).
• Color contrast compliant with 4.5:1 (normal text) and 3:1 (large text) ratio per WCAG AA.
• Links and buttons with descriptive text, not just "click here".
• Forms with labels associated to inputs, descriptive errors, and accessible status messages.
• Page structure with ARIA landmarks (banner, navigation, main, contentinfo).
• Screen reader support (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) with descriptive text.

sections.title: 5. Partial Compliance — Notable Limitations
sections.content:
The OG image SVG text may not be fully accessible to screen readers, as text content in SVG is not always read correctly. A text alternative is being developed.

The announcement bar uses pulse animations that, while supported by prefers-reduced-motion, may not be perfectly read by all screen readers. The textual content is accessible in any case.

Some card animations (hover effects, translate) use CSS transitions that may interfere with certain screen readers. The content remains accessible regardless of the animation.

sections.title: 6. Techniques Used for Compliance
sections.content:
• Semantic HTML (HTML5).
• WAI-ARIA 1.2 (Accessible Rich Internet Applications).
• CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) for layout and presentation.
• JavaScript (React) for interactivity — all interactive elements are keyboard and screen reader accessible.

sections.title: 7. Assessment Tools
sections.content:
The website has been evaluated using:

• Lighthouse Accessibility (Chrome DevTools).
• Axe DevTools (WAVE).
• Manual testing with keyboard and screen reader (VoiceOver on macOS).
• W3C Validator for HTML and CSS.

sections.title: 8. Feedback and Contact
sections.content:
If you encounter accessibility issues on this website, please contact me:

Email: riccardobozzato@gmail.com
Phone: +39 389 213 9542

I commit to responding within 5 business days and providing an alternative solution or the content in an accessible format (e.g., PDF, text document) within 15 business days.

sections.title: 9. Recourse Procedure
sections.content:
If my response is not satisfactory, you may contact:

AgID — Agency for Digital Italy
Office for Accessibility
https://www.agid.gov.it

AgID is the competent body for disputes regarding website accessibility in Italy.

sections.title: 10. Updates to This Statement
sections.content: This statement was last updated on July 21, 2026. It will be reviewed and updated periodically based on accessibility assessment results and regulatory changes.

home.heroTitle: Operations, delivery, and execution. With a builder mindset.
home.heroRole: No chaos. Just processes that work.
home.heroTagline: Delivery Manager & Head of Operations (PMP®). I build the systems — processes, KPIs, team structure — that let digital companies scale without imploding. €500K+ portfolio, teams of 8-12, -40% time-to-market. Open to work.
home.aboutTitle: From retail to the control room.
home.aboutText: 7+ years in operations. Started in retail (In's Mercato, Aldi), moved to Accenture (process automation, +30% efficiency), then Esse Solutions (€500K portfolio, teams of 8-12, Agile). Head of Operations at Ciao Elsa (2026). PMP® certified. Now I help digital teams scale without imploding.
home.freebieDesc: A 3-hour framework to map, measure, and prioritize operational debt — before it becomes a crisis.
home.servicesSubtitle: Four ways to bring order to your operations.

serviceList.title: Delivery & Process Design
serviceList.desc: Agile, Scrum, Kanban — but tailored to your team, not copied from a textbook. Release planning, capacity management, risk mitigation for teams of 8-12. 20-40% faster time-to-market.

serviceList.title: eCommerce & PIM-DAM Operations
serviceList.desc: Pimcore, Magento, Shopware — I've implemented them all. Enterprise integrations, platform migrations, data governance. 40% faster time-to-market on PIM projects.

serviceList.title: AI-Assisted Operations
serviceList.desc: Automate the boring stuff. Real-time KPI dashboards, centralized knowledge bases, AI workflows that actually work. Typical result: 10-40 hours/week recovered.

serviceList.title: Fractional Head of Ops / Delivery
serviceList.desc: For startups that aren't ready for a full-time Head of Operations — but need one anyway. Interim or part-time. I build the system, train your team, and hand over the keys.

projects.title: Case Studies
projects.subtitle: From concept to production — I built each one, start to finish. Real code, real deployments, real results.

panificio.title: Panificio Da Sergio
panificio.subtitle: Live: E-Commerce for a Family Bakery
panificio.description: Live at panificiodasergio.it — a full e-commerce platform for a traditional Italian bakery. Scope negotiation, stakeholder management across 3 generations, and shipping under a tight budget. Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and PostgreSQL.
features: Stakeholder management: translated 3 generations of family requirements into technical specs, balancing tradition with modern UX
Budget-constrained delivery: full e-commerce platform delivered under €2K material budget, using open-source stack and Netlify free tier
Multi-language (EN/IT) with local SEO for Italian and international customers
End-to-end order flow: product catalog → cart → pickup scheduling → confirmation → admin dashboard
Responsive design: mobile-first for on-the-go ordering, tablet-optimized for in-shop kiosk use
panificio.ctaDesc: Visit panificiodasergio.it to see the e-commerce platform in action — built for a real bakery, running in production.

vulnclaw.title: VulnClaw
vulnclaw.subtitle: Product Delivery: AI-Powered CLI from Zero to Open Source Community
vulnclaw.description: Built an open-source AI penetration testing CLI from scratch. Managed the full product lifecycle: scope definition, architecture design, community building, roadmap prioritization, and shipping v0.4.0 with active users across 3 continents. The tool integrates AI agents for autonomous reconnaissance, vulnerability scanning, and report generation. Key lesson: starting narrow beats building broad every time. 1,200+ GitHub stars, 40+ forks, 15+ community contributors.
features: Product scoping: evolved from a simple port scanner to a multi-agent AI penetration testing framework in 6 months (v0.1 → v0.4)
AI agent architecture: 3 specialized agents — Recon, Exploit, Report — that collaborate autonomously using LLM reasoning
Community management: triaged 80+ PRs and 200+ issues, maintaining a 48h first-response SLA for critical security reports
Roadmap prioritization: said 'no' to 60% of feature requests to ship the essential 40% — ruthless scope control
Open source as delivery strategy: MIT license, GitHub Discussions for roadmap voting, automated CI/CD with GitHub Actions

ric2brain.title: ric2brain
ric2brain.subtitle: Second Brain: AI-Native Knowledge Management
ric2brain.description: A 9,000+ note Obsidian vault connected to AI engines — semantic RAG (ChromaDB), LLM Wiki indexing, and Agent Memory. Every project, idea, lesson, and contact is indexed into a living knowledge graph, queryable in seconds by both humans and AI agents. Built with Obsidian, Python, ChromaDB, and MCP protocol integration.
features: Semantic RAG: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings turn every note into a vector — search by meaning, not keywords
LLM Wiki: structured index of all 9,000+ notes grouped by area for rapid AI agent navigation
Agent Memory: shared operational memory across AI sessions — agents log what they did and why
MCP Integration: vault exposed as Model Context Protocol resource, accessible from any compatible AI agent

shipkit.title: ShipKit
shipkit.subtitle: SaaS Kit: From Idea to €49 Paid Product
shipkit.description: Designed, built, and shipped a production-ready SaaS boilerplate (Next.js 16, Stripe, Auth, i18n) as a paid product. Managing the full delivery lifecycle: requirements gathering, scope control, payment integration, documentation, and customer support. The product ships with multi-tenant architecture, automated billing with Stripe webhooks, Google OAuth + magic link auth, and full i18n support. Sold at €49/one-time with lifetime updates. 12 real customers in the first 3 months.
features: Full product lifecycle: concept validation → architecture → build → ship → support → iteration
Scope control: 12-month ideal scope compressed to 8 months with ruthless MVP prioritization — saved 4 months of dev time
Payment integration: Stripe Checkout + webhooks + idempotency keys + graceful failure recovery for failed payments
Multi-tenant architecture: isolated tenant data, custom domains per tenant, shared infrastructure for cost efficiency
Internationalization: next-intl with full EN/IT support, RTL-ready, locale-aware routing baked in from day one
Documentation as delivery: 40+ pages of setup guides, API references, deployment walkthroughs, and troubleshooting

services.title: Operations & Delivery — Measurable Results
services.subtitle: Fixed price. Delivered.
services.heroDesc: You have an operations problem. Maybe your team is growing faster than your processes. Maybe you're firefighting instead of building. I design and implement the systems — process workflows, KPI frameworks, team structures — that fix it. PMP® certified, €500K+ delivered, teams of 8-12.

packages.title: Operations Audit
packages.desc: The entry point. Two-day analysis of your operational processes. You get a report with 3 immediate quick wins and a priority matrix. If you proceed with a full package, the audit cost is deducted.
features: Current-state process audit and flow mapping

packages.title: Delivery & Process Design
packages.desc: Stop firefighting. I'll help you set up the planning, capacity, and risk frameworks that let your team ship predictably. No Agile theater — just the practices that actually work for your team size.

packages.title: eCommerce & PIM-DAM Operations
packages.desc: Pimcore, Magento, Shopware — enterprise implementations, integrations, data governance. 20-40% faster time-to-market.

packages.title: AI-Assisted Operations
packages.desc: Operational workflow automation, real-time KPI dashboards, centralized knowledge management. Recover 10-40 hours/week.

packages.title: Fractional Head of Ops / Delivery
packages.desc: Interim or part-time for early-stage startups. I build the operational architecture, train the team, leave a working system.

steps.desc: I spend the first week inside your operations — talking to your team, mapping your workflows, finding the bottlenecks. You get a prioritized action plan, not a 50-page report.
I design the system you need: process flows, governance rules, metrics that matter. Everything is documented, everything has an owner.
I implement with weekly checkpoints and real progress updates. Your team works alongside me, so they own the system from day one.
Full documentation, trained team, measurable success criteria. Your system runs without me. Optional support if you need to iterate.

shipkit.title: ShipKit
shipkit.tagline: The SaaS kit that actually ships.
shipkit.description: Stop rebuilding auth, emails, billing, and i18n for every project. ShipKit gives you a production-ready foundation so you can focus on what makes your SaaS unique.

features.auth: Better Auth — magic links, OAuth (Google, GitHub), 2FA, session management
features.email: Resend + React Email — transactional emails, 5 templates included
features.i18n: next-intl — English and Italian out of the box, easy to extend
features.ui: shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 — dark mode, responsive, customizable

pricing.title: Simple Pricing
pricing.subtitle: One price. Everything included.
pricing.includes: Full source code, lifetime updates, priority support

freebie.title: Operational Chaos Diagnostic
freebie.subtitle: Three hours. A whiteboard. A clear picture of what's broken and how to fix it.
freebie.description: This is the same framework I use with my consulting clients — stripped of the consulting overhead. Map your workflows, baseline your metrics, and build a priority matrix in three focused hours. Works on a whiteboard or Miro.
whatsInside: The Process Map: end-to-end workflow visualization
The Metric Baseline: lead time, active time, wait time
The Priority Matrix: impact vs. fixability scoring
Top 3 Actions This Week: immediate next steps

form.success: Check your inbox! Click the confirmation link to get your diagnostic.

about.title: About Riccardo
about.subtitle: Operations & Delivery. PMP®. From retail to Head of Operations — with measurable results along the way.
about.intro: Delivery Manager & Head of Operations (PMP®). 7+ years making teams faster and less chaotic. Enterprise portfolio €500K+, teams of 8-12, 40% faster time-to-market. Started in retail, moved through Accenture, led operations at Ciao Elsa. PMP® certified. Now looking for the next challenge — if your team needs operational order, let's talk.
story: My career started in retail management — In’s Mercato, Aldi, Terranova. I learned operations the hard way: managing inventory, scheduling shifts, coaching teams, explaining to the regional manager why we missed target. It was the best possible training for delivery management, even if I didn’t know it yet.

Then Accenture. I built enterprise applications (SAP UI5, JavaScript) and automated processes that had been manual for years: +30% efficiency, -15% administrative errors, -20% response times. It was the first time I saw that the right process beats raw talent — every time.

At Esse Solutions I owned a €500K portfolio of digital projects, coordinating distributed teams of 8-12 across B2B eCommerce (Magento, Shopware) and PIM/DAM (Pimcore). I introduced structured Agile where there was none: productivity went up 25%, time-to-market dropped 40%, completion times shrank 20%.

At Ciao Elsa I was Head of Operations for 8 weeks — by design. The startup needed an operations function from scratch: processes, KPIs, team structure, governance. I built it, handed it over, and left. A short engagement that proved the approach works even at startup speed.

I'm currently in transition — my Ciao Elsa engagement has concluded and I'm open to new opportunities. I bring a strong track record: operational architectures built in both enterprise and startup environments, PMP® certification, and the conviction that the right process beats raw talent — every time. In the meantime, I build tools (ShipKit, VulnClaw) because you can't design good systems if you've never built anything.
about.philosophy: Most companies don't fail because they lack talent or ideas. They fail because their operations can't handle the weight of their own growth. I build the operating system that fixes that — combining the rigor of PMP® with the speed of someone who's been in the trenches. No jargon. No theater. Just systems that work.
about.ctaDesc: Have a project, idea, or just want to chat? I'm always open to interesting conversations.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how Riccardo Bozzato (“I”, “me”, “my”) collects, uses, and protects your personal data when you visit my website, download resources, engage my consulting services, or purchase ShipKit. I take your privacy seriously — I follow the GDPR (Reg. EU 2016/679) and the Italian Data Protection Code (D.Lgs. 196/2003, as amended by D.Lgs. 101/2018), and keep data collection to the minimum necessary for the requested services.

sections.title: 1. Data Controller
sections.content:
Riccardo Bozzato
Legnaro, PD, Italy
Email: riccardobozzato@gmail.com
Phone: +39 389 213 9542

sections.title: 2. Data Collected — Per Service
sections.content:
General contact form: name, email, and message. Used for role inquiries, projects, or consultations.

Operations Audit (€490): name, email, company, operational problem description. Collected to deliver the 2-day audit and prepare the report.

Delivery & Process Design (from €1,500): name, email, company, current team and process details. Required to design and implement the agreed operational framework.

eCommerce & PIM-DAM Operations (from €2,000): name, email, company, platform technical details (Magento, Shopware, Pimcore), temporary access credentials (if needed for implementation). Credentials are destroyed at engagement completion.

AI-Assisted Operations (from €2,500): name, email, company, process data and operational documentation. Used to design automations and dashboards.

Fractional Head of Ops (from €2,500/mo): name, email, company, organizational structure, financial and operational data required for the interim role.

CV download: no data collected — the download is anonymous.

ShipKit purchase (€49): name, email, billing address (via Stripe). I do not receive or store your credit card details — Stripe handles them on my behalf.

Freebie downloads (playbook): name and email address — used to deliver the PDF and send the email sequence (max 6 emails over 30 days).

Technical data: IP address, browser user-agent, and referral headers are automatically collected by the server for security and operational purposes. Not used for tracking or profiling.

Analytics: anonymous, aggregated page views using Plausible Analytics (privacy-friendly, no cookies). No personal tracking, no fingerprinting.

sections.title: 3. How I Use Your Data
sections.content:
Each data point is used exclusively for its stated purpose:

• Contact inquiries: to respond and prepare proposals.
• Consulting services (Audit, Delivery, eCommerce, AI Ops, Fractional): to deliver the agreed service, communicate during the engagement, and handle billing.
• ShipKit purchase: to process payment (via Stripe) and deliver the product.
• Playbook download: to send the PDF and associated educational email sequence.
• Technical data and analytics: to improve the site and detect technical issues.

I do not sell, rent, trade, or share your personal data with any third party for their marketing purposes. Your data is never used to train AI models.

sections.title: 4. Email Marketing (Consent, Double Opt-In & Unsubscribe)
sections.content:
When you download the free playbook, you may opt in (via a dedicated checkbox) to receive a 6-email educational sequence over approximately 30 days. After submitting the form, you will receive a confirmation email asking you to verify your address (double opt-in). You will start receiving the sequence only after confirming.

Each email includes an unsubscribe link. You can withdraw consent at any time — your request is processed immediately and you will receive no further emails. Unsubscribing does not affect communications related to active consulting engagements.

sections.title: 5. Legal Basis (GDPR) & Your Right to Object
sections.content:
I process your data based on:

Consent (Art. 6.1.a GDPR): when you check the consent box on a form (contact, playbook download, service request). You may withdraw at any time.

Contractual necessity (Art. 6.1.b GDPR): when you engage my consulting services or purchase ShipKit — processing is required to deliver the agreed work or product, including billing.

Legal obligation (Art. 6.1.c GDPR): retention of fiscal and accounting data for 10 years as required by Italian law (D.P.R. 600/73).

Legitimate interest (Art. 6.1.f GDPR): responding to pre-sales inquiries and improving my services.

Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR): where I process your data based on legitimate interest, you have the right to object at any time by contacting me. I will cease processing unless I have compelling legitimate grounds that override your interests.

sections.title: 6. Data Retention
sections.content:
Your data is retained only as long as necessary for the purpose collected:

• Contact form: deleted after 12 months from last contact.
• Operations Audit and single services: 24 months from engagement completion.
• Fractional Head of Ops (ongoing contracts): 24 months from contract end.
• ShipKit purchase: 10 years (Italian fiscal obligation).
• Playbook subscribers: until unsubscribe + 30 days grace period.
• Technical data (server logs): 30 days, then anonymized.

You may request early deletion of your data at any time by emailing riccardobozzato@gmail.com. Unless legally required (e.g., invoices), I will delete everything within 30 days.

sections.title: 7. Your Rights (GDPR)
sections.content:
You have the right to:

Access (Art. 15): request a copy of all data I hold about you.
Rectification (Art. 16): correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
Erasure (Art. 17): obtain deletion of your data (subject to legal retention requirements).
Restriction (Art. 18): restrict processing in certain cases.
Portability (Art. 20): receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format.
Object (Art. 21): object to processing based on legitimate interest.
Withdraw consent: at any time, without affecting the lawfulness of prior processing.

To exercise any right, email riccardobozzato@gmail.com. I will respond within 30 days (Art. 12 GDPR). If the request is complex, I will inform you within 30 days and may extend the deadline by up to 60 additional days.

sections.title: 8. Third-Party Processors
sections.content:
I use the following third-party services to operate this site and deliver services. Each has been verified for GDPR compliance. Data is processed within the EU and US (under Standard Contractual Clauses):

Resend (resend.com) — transactional email delivery, automated email sequences, and notifications. Privacy: resend.com/privacy — DPO: dpo@resend.com

Netlify (netlify.com) — website hosting, CDN, and deployment. Privacy: netlify.com/privacy — DPO: privacy@netlify.com

GitHub (github.com) — source code hosting and authentication. Privacy: github.com/privacy

Stripe (stripe.com) — payment processing for ShipKit purchases. Stripe is PCI DSS Level 1 certified. Privacy: stripe.com/privacy — DPO: dpo@stripe.com

Vercel (vercel.com) — deployment preview and serverless functions. Privacy: vercel.com/privacy

Plausible Analytics (plausible.io) — privacy-friendly analytics without cookies. Data is anonymized server-side. Privacy: plausible.io/privacy

sections.title: 9. International Transfers
sections.content: Some of my service providers (Stripe, GitHub, Resend) may transfer data to the United States. These transfers rely on Standard Contractual Clauses (SCC) approved by the European Commission (Implementing Decision 2021/914), which ensure a level of protection equivalent to the GDPR. You may request a copy of the applicable SCC by emailing riccardobozzato@gmail.com.

sections.title: 10. Data Security
sections.content: I implement appropriate technical and organizational measures: encrypted connections (HTTPS/TLS 1.3), access controls on databases, data minimization principle, and periodic security reviews. Temporary access credentials for client platform interventions are destroyed upon engagement completion. No data transmission over the internet is 100% secure, but I follow industry best practices to minimize risk.

sections.title: 11. Cookies
sections.content:
This website uses only:

• Technical cookies: required for site functionality (no consent required).
• Analytics cookies: Plausible Analytics does not use cookies — data is anonymized server-side.

No profiling, advertising, or cross-site tracking cookies are used. You can manage preferences via the cookie banner displayed on your first visit.

sections.title: 12. Job Application Data
sections.content: If you contact me regarding a job position (Head of Operations, Delivery Manager, Senior PM, etc.), the data you share (CV, portfolio, references) is used exclusively to evaluate your application and, if applicable, for the selection process. Data of non-selected candidates is deleted after 12 months.

sections.title: 13. Limitation of Liability
sections.content: Consulting services are provided with the professional diligence required by the PMP® Code of Ethics. However, results (e.g., time-to-market reduction, productivity gains) depend on external factors beyond my control (team cooperation, market conditions, technological constraints). Audit reports and recommendations provided are professional opinions based on facts at the time of analysis, not contractual guarantees of outcome.

sections.title: 14. Changes to This Policy
sections.content: I may update this privacy policy periodically to reflect regulatory changes, new services, or process modifications. Material changes will be communicated via email if I have your active contact, or via a notice on this website. The “Last updated” date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision.

sections.title: 15. Complaints
sections.content:
For any questions, requests, or complaints:

Riccardo Bozzato
Legnaro, PD, Italy
Email: riccardobozzato@gmail.com
Phone: +39 389 213 9542

If you believe the processing violates the GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Italian Data Protection Authority (Garante per la Protezione dei Dati Personali) at garanteprivacy.it — Piazza Venezia 11, 00187 Rome.

notFound.description: Either it never did, or I broke something. Either way, let's get you back on track.
